diagrams.title: Complete Graph K4 and its spanning trees
diagrams.description: Shows K4 with 4 labeled vertices and illustrates some of the 4^(4-2) = 16 possible spanning trees
elements: 16 different tree structures connecting all vertices
Examples: paths, stars, and other tree shapes
diagrams.purpose: Visualize Cayley's formula in action - 16 distinct labeled spanning trees in K4

diagrams.title: Prüfer Encoding Process
diagrams.description: Step-by-step showing how to derive a Prüfer sequence from a labeled tree by iteratively removing smallest leaf
elements: Step 1: Identify smallest leaf and its neighbor
diagrams.purpose: Understand the encoding algorithm for converting trees to Prüfer sequences

diagrams.title: Laplacian Matrix Construction
diagrams.description: Shows how to construct the Laplacian matrix from a simple graph and apply Matrix Tree Theorem
diagrams.purpose: Learn the step-by-step process of Kirchhoff's Matrix Tree Theorem application

diagrams.title: Types of Trees: Labeled vs Unlabeled
diagrams.description: Compares labeled and unlabeled trees for small values of n
elements: For n=2: 1 labeled tree (1 edge), 1 unlabeled tree
For n=3: 3 labeled trees (all permutations), 1 unlabeled tree (path of length 2)
For n=4: 16 labeled trees, 2 unlabeled trees (path and star)
Visual distinction between structure and labeling
diagrams.purpose: Understand the difference between counting trees with and without vertex labels
